questions.question: What is the key difference between VO and VA on the DA42-VI?

options.text: VO is higher than VA and allows more aggressive maneuvering
They are identical speeds with different naming conventions
VO is more restrictive — at or below VO, a nose-up pitch will stall the wing before exceeding structural limits
VA is the speed used in the DA42-VI; VO is not placarded

questions.explanation: VO (Operating Maneuvering Speed) is more restrictive than VA. At or below VO, pulling back on the stick will stall the wing before exceeding structural limits. The DA42-VI placards VO, not VA.

questions.question: What is the maximum takeoff mass of the ERAU DA42-VI with MAM 42-678 carried out?
questions.explanation: With MAM 42-678 carried out, the maximum takeoff mass is 4,407 lbs (1,999 kg). This is the limit for all ERAU DA42-VI aircraft.

questions.question: What is the maximum demonstrated crosswind component with flaps in the APP position?
questions.explanation: The maximum demonstrated crosswind component is 20 knots with flaps APP and 25 knots with flaps UP.

questions.question: How does the forward CG limit change as the DA42-VI's weight increases?

options.text: It moves forward, becoming less restrictive
It moves aft, becoming more restrictive
It remains constant regardless of weight
It oscillates depending on fuel load

questions.explanation: The forward CG limit moves aft as weight increases. At minimum flight mass it is 92.52 inches aft of datum; at maximum takeoff mass it moves to 95.83 inches aft of datum.

questions.question: What is the maximum operating altitude for the DA42-VI?
questions.explanation: The DA42-VI has a maximum operating altitude of 18,000 feet pressure altitude. Above this altitude, the aircraft is not certified for flight.
